name: (a) per-hole match NET — clean win. Subject A (Rick) wins 5 holes, loses 2, ties 11 → 3 up; B's stakeholder pays A's 3 × stake.
_contract: Golden HAND-CALC fixture for the NEW bets engine per_hole_match type (engine/bets/). Per-hole MATCH PLAY (FR12): each scoped hole is graded independently — LOWER value wins that hole (+1 to that side), an equal hole is a PUSH (contributes zero). The bet's money is (holesWonA − holesWonB) × stake (NOT winner-take-stake like h2h; the stake is per hole of MARGIN). The engine CONSUMES per-hole values as a GIVEN input (P2 — it never re-derives them; netForSegment is validated separately). `netPerHoleBySubject` holds the per-hole values for the bet's basis (net here); the field name is shared with h2h. `expected` is hand-calculated truth the engine must match. SettlementEdge direction: fromPlayerId PAYS toPlayerId (debtor → creditor). Money in integer cents. result.marginNet here is the HOLE margin (|holesWonA − holesWonB|), and subjectNetTotal holds each subject's HOLES WON (reusing the shared SettlementOutcome shape; no spine change).
_handCalc: Grade each hole, lower net wins it. Holes 1–5: Rick 3 vs Ben 4 → Rick wins (5 holes). Holes 6–7: Rick 4 vs Ben 3 → Ben wins (2 holes). Holes 8–18: 4 vs 4 → push (11 holes, zero). Rick wins 5, Ben wins 2 → Rick is 5 − 2 = 3 up. Money = margin × stake = 3 × $5.00 = $15.00 = 1500c. Loser's stakeholder (Ben) pays winner's stakeholder (Rick). Exactly one SettlementEdge: ben → rick, 1500c.
